meaning: n.鍥版儜,杩蜂贡,涓洪毦,鎱屽紶
examples: He shook his head in bewilderment at the obduracy of this man.
During the confusion and bewilderment of the second day Mary hid herself in the nursery and was forgotten by everyone.
Much to the bewilderment of professional scientists, quasi-religious cults are being formed around such unlikely topics as quantum physics, space-time relativity, black holes and the big bang.
Indeed, one might speak of the Four Stages of the Barnes Experience: Bewilderment, Curiosity, Insight, Appreciation.
Shaking my head in bewilderment, it occurred to me: Could this be a red herring?
